stories.title: Iran warns advanced weapons not yet deployed in conflict
stories.description: Tehran's Defense Ministry claims it has reserved its most powerful systems, rejecting Trump's assertion that Iran's military has been neutralized.

stories.title: Ship hit near UAE's Fujairah as Gulf attacks escalate
stories.description: The latest strike is part of a wave of attacks on commercial shipping since U.S.-Israeli operations against Iran began on Feb. 28.

stories.title: Poll finds half of Americans now support abolishing ICE
stories.description: Support hit 50% for the first time in YouGov polling, with opposition falling to an all-time low of 39%, amid fallout from deadly shootings.

stories.title: Trump security team briefs Congress ahead of Iran war powers votes
stories.description: The Senate is set to vote Wednesday on a resolution requiring congressional approval for further military action against Iran.

stories.title: Turkey engaging all sides to end Iran war, foreign minister says
stories.description: Foreign Minister Fidan said Ankara is coordinating with Oman, European nations, and the U.S. to halt the conflict and revive negotiations.

stories.title: Texas, North Carolina hold first Senate primaries of 2026 midterm cycle
stories.description: Voters cast ballots Tuesday in high-stakes races as the U.S. military campaign against Iran enters its fourth day.

stories.title: Pentagon identifies four US troops killed in Iranian drone strike
stories.description: All four Army Reserve soldiers died when a low-flying drone evaded air defenses and hit a makeshift operations center at a Kuwait port.

stories.title: Merz meets Trump as Germany's military buildup reshapes Europe
stories.description: Germany's chancellor visited the White House as Berlin's plan to more than double defense spending draws both applause and anxiety from European allies.

stories.title: Survey finds one in three European women face violence
stories.description: A sweeping new survey of more than 114,000 women across all 27 EU member states found that most cases of violence go unreported.

stories.title: US sends military, charter flights to evacuate citizens from Middle East
stories.description: The State Department said it is securing military aircraft and charter flights after Iranian strikes crippled airports in Dubai, Abu Dhabi, and Doha.

stories.title: Moderate House Democrats introduce rival Iran war powers resolution
stories.description: Six centrist Democrats proposed a 30-day deadline for Iran operations, splitting from a stricter bipartisan measure headed to a floor vote Thursday.

stories.title: Syria deploys thousands of troops to Lebanon border
stories.description: The buildup, which began in February and accelerated recently, aims to block Hezbollah infiltration, according to Reuters.

stories.title: CIA working to arm Kurdish forces for Iran uprising
stories.description: The effort aims to spark an uprising as the U.S.-Israeli military campaign against Iran enters its second week with no clear endgame.

stories.title: Iran threatens to attack any ship in Strait of Hormuz as Asia scrambles for energy
stories.description: Asian governments are racing to secure alternative oil and gas supplies after Iran declared the strait closed, sending LNG prices surging.

stories.title: Dr. Oz opens fraud probe into New York's Medicaid program
stories.description: The CMS administrator sent Gov. Hochul 50 questions about cost controls and provider oversight in the state's $124 billion program.

stories.title: Iran's Assembly of Experts elects Mojtaba Khamenei as next supreme leader
stories.description: The slain leader's son was reportedly chosen under Revolutionary Guard pressure, even as Israeli airstrikes struck the vote site in Qom.

stories.title: Sudan accuses Ethiopia of drone attacks in its civil war
stories.description: Khartoum says drones launched from Ethiopian territory struck targets inside Sudan, the first time it has directly implicated its neighbor in the conflict.

stories.title: Iranian drone strikes US Consulate in Dubai as war widens across Gulf
stories.description: The attack came as Iran targeted American embassies and military bases across the region in retaliation for the killing of Ayatollah Khamenei.

stories.title: Israel says it destroyed secret Iranian nuclear site near Tehran
stories.description: The IDF said it struck a covert underground facility where scientists were developing nuclear weapons components, the first such site revealed since June 2025.

stories.title: Trump says worst outcome in Iran is a new leader 'as bad' as Khamenei
stories.description: The president acknowledged most U.S.-identified successors to the slain supreme leader have already been killed in ongoing strikes.

stories.title: Macron deploys aircraft carrier to Mediterranean amid Middle East crisis
stories.description: France ordered the Charles de Gaulle carrier group to the Mediterranean and deployed air defenses to Cyprus after a drone struck a British base there.

stories.title: Lutnick volunteers to testify on Epstein ties
stories.description: The commerce secretary's offer comes as lawmakers were preparing a bipartisan subpoena to compel his appearance before the House Oversight Committee.

stories.title: Top U.S. general details timeline of Iran strikes
stories.description: General Dan Caine outlined how the coordinated U.S.-Israel assault on Iran unfolded, as the conflict enters its fourth day with four U.S. troops killed.

stories.title: Ukraine refuses EU requests to inspect damaged Druzhba pipeline
stories.description: Kyiv denied access to the EU ambassador and top EU leaders, citing security concerns, as Hungary leverages the dispute to block a €90 billion loan.

stories.title: FCC chair says Paramount-WBD merger will be approved 'quickly'
stories.description: FCC Chairman Brendan Carr told CNBC the deal is "cleaner" than Netflix's earlier bid, signaling a smoother regulatory path for the $110 billion acquisition.

stories.title: Trump orders Navy escorts, federal insurance for Gulf tankers
stories.description: The president directed the U.S. Development Finance Corporation to offer political risk coverage for ships transiting the Strait of Hormuz amid the Iran conflict.

stories.title: Apple unveils six devices in two days, more expected
stories.description: Pre-orders for the M5 MacBook Air, M5 Pro/Max MacBook Pro, new Studio Displays, iPhone 17e, and M4 iPad Air open March 4.

stories.title: Oil tops $82 as Iran conflict threatens Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: Brent crude hit its highest level since January 2025 as the U.S.-Israeli military campaign against Iran disrupted shipping through a vital global oil chokepoint.

stories.title: GOP Sen. Tillis calls for Noem's resignation at hearing
stories.description: The North Carolina Republican invoked Noem's memoir about killing her dog to illustrate what he called a pattern of failed leadership at DHS.

stories.title: IAEA confirms damage at Iran's Natanz nuclear site
stories.description: The U.N. nuclear watchdog said satellite imagery shows entrance buildings were hit but no radiological consequences are expected.

stories.title: Supreme Court lets California schools tell parents about child's gender identity
stories.description: The 6-3 ruling reinstates a federal injunction blocking state laws that limited schools from disclosing students' transgender status to parents.

stories.title: DOJ reverses itself, seeks to revive Trump law firm cases
stories.description: Hours after moving to drop appeals of rulings that struck down Trump's executive orders targeting four law firms, the Justice Department reversed course.

stories.title: Israel sends ground forces into southern Lebanon amid Hezbollah clashes
stories.description: The IDF said troops seized strategic positions beyond the border to create a security buffer as fighting with Hezbollah escalates alongside the broader Iran conflict.

stories.title: Ukraine says it recaptured more land than Russia seized in February
stories.description: Kyiv's top general called it the first month Ukraine outpaced Russia in territorial gains since the 2024 Kursk incursion.

stories.title: Judge rules Trump bid to kill NYC congestion pricing unlawful
stories.description: U.S. District Judge Lewis Liman found the Transportation Department's 2025 move to terminate Manhattan's first-in-the-nation tolling program exceeded its authority.

stories.title: Judge blocks ICE visit restrictions for all of Congress
stories.description: A federal judge struck down DHS's seven-day notice requirement for the third time, now extending the ruling to protect all lawmakers, not just the 13 who sued.

stories.title: House panel releases Clinton's Epstein deposition videos
stories.description: More than nine hours of footage show Bill and Hillary Clinton answering questions about Jeffrey Epstein after months of legal battles.

stories.title: Russia's Medvedev warns of World War III over US strikes on Iran
stories.description: The deputy chairman of Russia's Security Council called Trump's regime-change campaign "a grave mistake" that has put all Americans at risk.

stories.title: Israel to reopen Kerem Shalom crossing for Gaza aid
stories.description: COGAT announced the key cargo crossing will reopen Tuesday after a days-long closure triggered by joint U.S.-Israeli strikes on Iran.

stories.title: Supreme Court blocks redrawing of NYC's only GOP district
stories.description: The ruling preserves Rep. Nicole Malliotakis's Staten Island-based seat for 2026, halting a state court order that found the map dilutes minority votes.

stories.title: Noem refuses to retract 'terrorist' label for slain citizens
stories.description: At a Senate hearing, the DHS secretary denied calling Alex Pretti a "domestic terrorist," saying she described the incident, not the person.

stories.title: Rubio says Israel's strike plan triggered US attack on Iran
stories.description: The secretary of state said the U.S. struck Iran preemptively after learning Israel would attack, fearing Tehran's automatic retaliation against American forces.

stories.title: Bolivia raids homes to recover cash looted from deadly plane crash
stories.description: Security forces searched at least 22 homes near El Alto, where a military plane carrying $62 million in new banknotes crashed last week, killing 22.

stories.title: Trump says he'll attend Correspondents' Dinner for first time as president
stories.description: The announcement ends a years-long boycott of the annual press dinner, which Trump skipped throughout his entire first term.

stories.title: Montana Rep. Zinke won't seek reelection, adding to GOP retirement wave
stories.description: The former Navy SEAL and Trump Interior secretary cited lingering military injuries, becoming the latest of more than 30 House Republicans to step aside.

stories.title: Saudi Arabia goes on full military alert after Iranian drone strikes
stories.description: The GCC condemned Iran's attacks across all six member states and invoked its joint defense pact, reserving the right to retaliate.

stories.title: Trump won't rule out ground troops in Iran as strikes enter third day
stories.description: Six U.S. service members have been killed in Iranian retaliatory strikes as the Pentagon declines to outline an exit strategy.

stories.title: Sanders and Khanna introduce 5% wealth tax on billionaires
stories.description: The bill would tax America's 938 billionaires to fund $3,000 direct payments to most households and expand social programs.

stories.title: Navy intel chief warns China could strike US from its own waters by 2030s
stories.description: New submarines armed with advanced ballistic missiles will be able to target large portions of the U.S. from Chinese coastal waters, Rear Adm. Brookes told Congress.

stories.title: Netanyahu lobbied Trump for months to launch Iran war
stories.description: The detailed account describes the U.S. decision to strike Iran as a major victory for Netanyahu, who first raised the idea in December.

stories.title: Supreme Court hears case on gun ban for marijuana users
stories.description: The justices weighed whether the federal law barring "unlawful" drug users from possessing firearms violates the Second Amendment.

stories.title: Macron offers to station French nuclear weapons in Europe
stories.description: France will expand its arsenal and extend "forward deterrence" to European allies, marking the biggest shift in French nuclear doctrine in decades.

stories.title: Rubio says U.S. to roll out energy cost relief amid Iran oil crisis
stories.description: Treasury and Energy secretaries will unveil measures Tuesday to cushion Americans from surging fuel prices after Iran conflict disrupted global oil supply.

stories.title: Putin calls Gulf leaders as Iran conflict widens
stories.description: Russia says it is in "constant contact" with Tehran and Gulf states as U.S.-Israeli strikes on Iran enter a third day.

stories.title: Three US F-15s shot down by Kuwaiti air defenses in friendly fire incident
stories.description: All six crew members ejected safely and are in stable condition, CENTCOM said, as the Iran conflict enters its fourth day.

stories.title: Treasury ends Anthropic AI use as agencies comply with Trump ban
stories.description: Federal housing agencies also cut ties with the AI company, which refused to drop safety guardrails on surveillance and autonomous weapons for the Pentagon.

stories.title: Epstein files show DOJ examined accused men but charged none
stories.description: Newly released documents reveal victims cooperated with investigators after Epstein's 2019 death, but prosecutors cited insufficient evidence to bring cases.

stories.title: CIA tracked Khamenei for months before Israel struck Tehran
stories.description: U.S. intelligence identified a rare gathering of Iran's top leaders, enabling the strike that killed the supreme leader and over 40 senior officials.

stories.title: Trump pushes Venezuela model for Iran transition
stories.description: The president told The New York Times he has "three very good choices" for Iran's next leader but declined to name them.

stories.title: Epstein files show doctors shared women's health records with him
stories.description: More than 15,000 newly released documents reveal how elite physicians bent ethical rules to serve the sex offender and the women he controlled.

stories.title: US confirms first combat use of drone reverse-engineered from Iran's Shahed
stories.description: The LUCAS kamikaze drone, built by copying a captured Iranian Shahed-136, was deployed in Operation Epic Fury strikes on Iranian military targets.

stories.title: Iran's foreign minister vows 'no limit' to self-defense after Khamenei killed
stories.description: Araghchi rejected Trump's warning against retaliation, saying Iran's military capability is intact and its forces are already striking back.

stories.title: UN Security Council holds emergency session on US-Israel strikes against Iran
stories.description: Iran retaliated with missile attacks across the region after the joint operation dubbed "Operation Epic Fury" targeted sites in multiple Iranian cities.

stories.title: OpenAI reaches deal to deploy AI on Pentagon's classified networks
stories.description: The agreement, announced by CEO Sam Altman, includes safety red lines on mass surveillance and autonomous weapons that rival Anthropic fought for but couldn't secure.

stories.title: Businesses demand $175B in tariff refunds as Trump touts economy in Texas
stories.description: A coalition of 950 businesses pressed the president during his Corpus Christi visit, saying Texas small businesses alone are owed $11 billion in unlawful tariff costs.

stories.title: Saudi, UAE leaders unite against Iran after missiles rock Gulf capitals
stories.description: Saudi Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman pledged "full solidarity" with UAE President Sheikh Mohammed bin Zayed after Iranian strikes killed one in Abu Dhabi.

stories.title: DOJ won't appeal ruling barring death penalty for Mangione
stories.description: Federal prosecutors said Friday they will not challenge a judge's decision removing the death penalty from Luigi Mangione's case, clearing the way for trial.

stories.title: Trump launched Iran strikes despite intel doubting his missile threat claims
stories.description: Intelligence sources told Reuters that Trump's claim Iran could "soon" hit the U.S. with missiles was unsupported by assessments putting that capability a decade away.

stories.title: Houthis to resume Red Sea attacks after U.S.-Israeli strikes on Iran
stories.description: Two senior officials told the AP the group's first strike could come as soon as "tonight," ending a ceasefire with Washington.

stories.title: Chile bars Chinese hospital ship from treating locals amid US tensions
stories.description: The decision came as Washington ramps up pressure on Santiago over a proposed Chinese undersea cable project.

stories.title: Trump asks if Supreme Court can rehear tariff case
stories.description: The president called the 6-3 ruling "highly disappointing" and warned it could force refunds of hundreds of billions to foreign companies.

stories.title: Iran's defense chief, IRGC commander killed in US-Israeli strikes
stories.description: Joint US-Israeli military operations struck Tehran and other Iranian cities Saturday, killing top military leaders and triggering Iranian missile retaliation.

stories.title: Pentagon bars troops from Ivy League graduate programs
stories.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth ordered the cancellation of all military attendance at Princeton, Columbia, MIT, Brown, Yale, and other elite schools starting next academic year.

stories.title: Classified report found Noem's shoe policy created airport security risks
stories.description: A DHS inspector general report described risks from the policy as "significant," according to The Wall Street Journal, adding to scrutiny of Noem's leadership.

stories.title: Russia condemns US-Israeli strikes on Iran, evacuates citizens
stories.description: Moscow labeled the joint military operation "unprovoked armed aggression," warned of radiological catastrophe, and began evacuating Russians from Iran.

stories.title: Ghana says 55 citizens killed fighting for Russia in Ukraine
stories.description: Foreign Minister Ablakwa said 272 Ghanaians were lured into the conflict since 2022 through criminal trafficking networks, with two held as POWs.

stories.title: Trump says he's considering Cruz for Supreme Court
stories.description: The president revived the idea at a Texas rally, despite Cruz rejecting it last month, saying "hell no" to any nomination.

stories.title: FAA to cut O'Hare flights, citing overscheduling by major airlines
stories.description: United Airlines and American Airlines have dramatically ramped up summer schedules at the Chicago hub, prompting federal intervention.

stories.title: Slovak PM says Ukraine unwilling to restart Druzhba oil flow
stories.description: Slovakia's prime minister spoke with Zelenskyy on Friday but said Kyiv rejected a joint inspection proposal, deepening a dispute that has stalled EU sanctions and aid.

stories.title: Iran's top security chief rejects talks with US as strikes enter third day
stories.description: Ali Larijani said Tehran "will not negotiate" with Washington, rebuffing Trump's claim that Iran's post-Khamenei leadership wanted dialogue.

stories.title: Pentagon laser accidentally downs a Border Patrol drone in Texas
stories.description: The military mistakenly shot down a CBP drone near Fort Hancock, marking the second border laser incident this month and drawing Democratic accusations of incompetence.

stories.title: DOJ indicts 30 more over anti-ICE church protest in Minnesota
stories.description: Attorney General Bondi said 25 of the newly charged defendants have been arrested, bringing the total number indicted in the case to 39.

stories.title: Judge blocks early end of SAVE student loan plan
stories.description: A federal judge dismissed the Trump administration's bid to eliminate the SAVE repayment plan, keeping the program alive for over 7 million borrowers.

stories.title: Satellite images reveal US military buildup at Saudi airbase
stories.description: Aircraft at Prince Sultan Airbase jumped from 27 to 43 over four days in February as the US ramps up its largest Middle East deployment in two decades.

stories.title: ACLU sues Kansas over law invalidating transgender IDs
stories.description: The lawsuit challenges SB 244, which retroactively voided driver's licenses and birth certificates of transgender residents who had legally updated their gender markers.

stories.title: Trump denies knowledge of draft emergency order on midterm elections
stories.description: The president said he had "never heard about" a proposal to declare a national emergency to reshape voting rules before November's midterms.

stories.title: US birthrate hits record low, driven by teens and young women
stories.description: Nearly half of American women at age 30 are now childless, as the steepest fertility declines fall on the youngest and least affluent.

stories.title: NASA overhauls Artemis moon program, adds test flight before lunar landing
stories.description: Administrator Isaacman said a new 2027 orbital mission will test commercial landers before attempting up to two moon landings in 2028.

stories.title: Court lets Trump end union bargaining for federal workers
stories.description: The Ninth Circuit vacated an injunction blocking the president's executive order, which strips collective bargaining rights from hundreds of thousands of employees.

stories.title: OpenAI raises record $110B from Amazon, Nvidia, SoftBank
stories.description: The funding round, at a $730 billion valuation, is the largest private financing deal ever and includes a sweeping Amazon partnership.

stories.title: Zelenskyy says he's ready to meet Putin for peace talks
stories.description: In a Sky News interview, the Ukrainian president said there is a closing window to end the war before U.S. midterm elections in November.

stories.title: Trump tells homebuyers to 'wait a little longer' for lower mortgage rates
stories.description: The president urged patience on housing costs as rates hit a three-year low but sales slumped to their weakest pace in over two years.

stories.title: Asian shares fall as Nvidia slump and Pakistan-Afghanistan war rattle markets
stories.description: Investors fled to safe havens Friday as tech valuations sagged post-Nvidia earnings and Pakistan declared "open war" on Afghanistan.

stories.title: Nintendo plans $1.9B share sale as banks unwind stakes
stories.description: MUFG Bank and the Bank of Kyoto are expected to sell their strategic holdings in the gaming giant, with a buyback also planned.

stories.title: Senate Democrats demand tariff refunds, accuse Bessent of 'stonewalling'
stories.description: More than two dozen senators urged the Treasury to begin repaying billions in duties the Supreme Court struck down last week.

stories.title: Nvidia drops sharply despite record earnings as Block cuts half its staff
stories.description: Wall Street sold off Thursday as Nvidia's blowout quarter failed to satisfy investors, while Block announced 4,000 layoffs tied to AI.

stories.title: Green Party wins first-ever UK by-election, pushing Labour to third
stories.description: Hannah Spencer captured the Gorton and Denton seat in Greater Manchester, overturning a Labour majority in a district the party held for nearly a century.

stories.title: Trump floats 'friendly takeover of Cuba' amid oil blockade
stories.description: The president said Cuba is "in a big deal of trouble" and that Secretary of State Rubio is negotiating at a "very high level."

stories.title: Germany votes to let military intercept drones
stories.description: The Bundestag passed a law allowing the Bundeswehr to jam or shoot down drones, responding to a surge in suspicious flights linked to Russia.

stories.title: Venezuela suspends 19 Maduro-era oil contracts
stories.description: Caracas and Washington are reviewing the deals and may revoke some, as both governments scrutinize the credentials of little-known firms involved.

stories.title: Meloni calls Trump tariffs a 'mistake,' proposes EU-US free-trade zone
stories.description: Italy's prime minister urged the EU to push for zero tariffs with Washington as the Turnberry trade deal remains frozen after a Supreme Court ruling.

stories.title: White House confirms American killed in Cuba speedboat shootout
stories.description: At least one U.S. citizen died and another was arrested after Cuban forces fired on a stolen Florida boat whose occupants allegedly shot first.

stories.title: Maduro asks judge to toss drug indictment over blocked legal fees
stories.description: His lawyer argues the U.S. violated Maduro's right to counsel by blocking Venezuela from paying his defense costs.

stories.title: GOP senators join Democrats demanding DOJ release Epstein files tied to Trump
stories.description: Bipartisan pressure mounted Thursday after an NPR investigation found FBI interview summaries involving accusations against the president were missing from the public release.

stories.title: Syria, Druze factions complete first prisoner swap since summer clashes
stories.description: The exchange of 86 detainees in Sweida marks a rare step toward political resolution in one of Syria's last autonomous regions.

stories.title: 171 bodies found in mass graves near Congo's Uvira after M23 withdrawal
stories.description: Officials say two graves were discovered in areas the Rwanda-backed rebel group recently vacated amid escalating violence in eastern Congo.

stories.title: NYC mayor pitches Trump on $21B housing plan in Queens
stories.description: Mamdani presented a mock Daily News front page reading "Trump to City: Let's Build" to sell the proposal, and Trump was "very enthusiastic," his office said.

stories.title: Russia says it downed 220 Ukrainian drones over nine hours
stories.description: The interceptions over nine hours included 27 drones heading for Moscow, according to Russian officials, amid escalating aerial exchanges on both sides.

stories.title: ICE struggles to vet recruits amid hiring surge, internal email shows
stories.description: An internal email warned supervisors that delayed background checks are creating confusion as some recruits were flagged for gang ties and active warrants.

stories.title: US and Congo sign $1.2B health deal amid African data-sharing concerns
stories.description: The DRC became the 19th country to sign a bilateral health agreement under Trump's "America First Global Health Strategy," even as the Africa CDC raised alarm over data provisions.

stories.title: UN denounces Taliban decree expanding executions and curbing rights
stories.description: Volker Türk condemned a new Taliban decree expanding the death penalty and legalizing corporal punishment, likening women's treatment to gender apartheid.

stories.title: US offers $10M reward for tips leading to capture of Sinaloa Cartel leaders
stories.description: The State Department announced bounties for René and Alfonso Arzate-García as a superseding indictment charges them with narcoterrorism.

stories.title: FBI's congressional affairs chief departs amid ongoing bureau turmoil
stories.description: Marshall Yates, a key liaison between the FBI and Congress who helped carry out the Trump administration's retribution efforts, is leaving this week.

stories.title: Google rolls out Nano Banana 2 as default AI image model across Gemini
stories.description: The upgrade brings Pro-level image generation to all users at faster speeds, spanning the Gemini app, Google Search, and 141 countries.

stories.title: US saw net negative migration in 2025, a first since the Great Depression
stories.description: A Wall Street Journal investigation found at least 180,000 Americans relocated abroad last year as the nation lost more people than it gained.

stories.title: White House says Trump open to Kim talks 'without preconditions'
stories.description: The response came hours after Kim Jong Un signaled willingness to engage at the close of North Korea's most significant political congress in five years.

stories.title: Hillary Clinton's Epstein deposition paused after Boebert leaks photo
stories.description: Rep. Lauren Boebert allegedly gave an unauthorized photo from the closed-door hearing to commentator Benny Johnson, who posted it online.

stories.title: Stellantis cuts UAW profit-sharing for first time in 15 years after record loss
stories.description: The automaker reported a net loss of roughly $26.3 billion for 2025, driven by massive EV writedowns and a sweeping strategic reset.

stories.title: Columbia student detained by ICE freed after NYC mayor raised case with Trump
stories.description: Ellie Aghayeva, a senior from Azerbaijan, was arrested from a campus building after agents allegedly posed as missing-person investigators.

stories.title: US, Ukraine meet in Geneva after Russia's massive overnight aerial barrage
stories.description: Talks focused on a postwar "prosperity package" and preparations for trilateral negotiations with Russia expected in early March.

stories.title: Rutgers study challenges theory that melting glaciers could slow climate change
stories.description: Meltwater from an Antarctic ice shelf delivers far less iron to the ocean than assumed, undermining a key natural climate feedback mechanism.

stories.title: Trump surgeon general pick lacks GOP votes after vaccine grilling
stories.description: At least two Republican senators remain undecided on Casey Means after she sidestepped vaccine questions at her confirmation hearing.

stories.title: US rare earth shortages deepen as suppliers halt production
stories.description: Two North American aerospace coating firms have suspended operations and begun rationing yttrium amid a 69-fold price surge over the past year.

stories.title: UN issues first carbon credits under Paris Agreement
stories.description: The milestone approval involves a clean-cooking project in Myanmar, though environmental groups have questioned the methodology behind the credits.

stories.title: US aims to process 4,500 white South African refugees per month
stories.description: A contracting document reveals the White House's monthly target far exceeds Trump's annual refugee cap of 7,500 for all nationalities.

stories.title: Space Force halts Vulcan rocket launches over booster anomaly
stories.description: The suspension could last "many months," jeopardizing a GPS satellite launch next month and ULA's plans to fly up to 22 Vulcan missions this year.

stories.title: DARPA awards RTX contract for kilometer-range X-ray vision
stories.description: The XENA program aims to let military commanders identify concealed threats from nearly a kilometer away, far beyond current X-ray scanner range.

stories.title: Trump admin defers $259M in Medicaid funds from Minnesota
stories.description: Vice President Vance and CMS Administrator Dr. Oz announced the action as part of a new "war on fraud" declared during Trump's State of the Union.

stories.title: Nearly blind refugee found dead in Buffalo after Border Patrol left him
stories.description: Nurul Amin Shah Alam, 56, went missing for six days after agents dropped him off without contacting his family or lawyer. Buffalo's mayor called the death "preventable."

stories.title: NASA's Fincke says he was behind historic ISS medical evacuation
stories.description: The veteran astronaut broke weeks of silence, revealing he experienced a "medical event" on Jan. 7 that cut the Crew-11 mission short.

stories.title: Labor Dept. proposes rule easing gig worker classification
stories.description: The proposal would rescind a Biden-era standard and make it easier for companies to treat workers as independent contractors.

stories.title: Canada sends $8M in food aid to Cuba amid US fuel blockade
stories.description: Mexico also dispatched a second shipment of humanitarian supplies as the island faces what the UN has warned could be a humanitarian collapse.

stories.title: Bipartisan lawmakers press Navy to finalize 15-submarine deal
stories.description: Congress warns that further delays to the multiyear contract for Virginia-class and Columbia-class boats could destabilize the nuclear submarine industrial base.

stories.title: Patel fires FBI staff tied to Trump probe, says phone records seized
stories.description: The FBI director ordered the ouster of at least 10 employees and revealed the bureau had subpoenaed his and Susie Wiles' call records during the Biden era.

stories.title: Largest-ever ALMA image reveals Milky Way's hidden core
stories.description: The mosaic spans more than 650 light-years, mapping cold star-forming gases and dozens of molecules near the galaxy's central black hole.

stories.title: Larry Summers to resign from Harvard over Epstein ties
stories.description: The former Harvard president and Treasury secretary will give up his University Professorship, the school's highest faculty distinction, at the end of the academic year.

stories.title: Trump says Omar and Tlaib should be 'sent back' after State of the Union clash
stories.description: The president called the two Muslim American lawmakers "lunatics" on Truth Social a day after they heckled him during his address to Congress.

stories.title: Environmental groups sound alarm after Colorado River talks miss another deadline
stories.description: Seven western states failed to agree on sharing the river's dwindling supply before current rules expire, as historic snow drought drains reservoirs.

top.title: Iran warns advanced weapons not yet deployed in conflict
top.description: Tehran's Defense Ministry claims it has reserved its most powerful systems, rejecting Trump's assertion that Iran's military has been neutralized.

top.title: Ship hit near UAE's Fujairah as Gulf attacks escalate
top.description: The latest strike is part of a wave of attacks on commercial shipping since U.S.-Israeli operations against Iran began on Feb. 28.

top.title: Poll finds half of Americans now support abolishing ICE
top.description: Support hit 50% for the first time in YouGov polling, with opposition falling to an all-time low of 39%, amid fallout from deadly shootings.

top.title: Trump security team briefs Congress ahead of Iran war powers votes
top.description: The Senate is set to vote Wednesday on a resolution requiring congressional approval for further military action against Iran.

top.title: Turkey engaging all sides to end Iran war, foreign minister says
top.description: Foreign Minister Fidan said Ankara is coordinating with Oman, European nations, and the U.S. to halt the conflict and revive negotiations.

top.title: Texas, North Carolina hold first Senate primaries of 2026 midterm cycle
top.description: Voters cast ballots Tuesday in high-stakes races as the U.S. military campaign against Iran enters its fourth day.

top.title: Pentagon identifies four US troops killed in Iranian drone strike
top.description: All four Army Reserve soldiers died when a low-flying drone evaded air defenses and hit a makeshift operations center at a Kuwait port.

top.title: Merz meets Trump as Germany's military buildup reshapes Europe
top.description: Germany's chancellor visited the White House as Berlin's plan to more than double defense spending draws both applause and anxiety from European allies.

top.title: Survey finds one in three European women face violence
top.description: A sweeping new survey of more than 114,000 women across all 27 EU member states found that most cases of violence go unreported.

top.title: US sends military, charter flights to evacuate citizens from Middle East
top.description: The State Department said it is securing military aircraft and charter flights after Iranian strikes crippled airports in Dubai, Abu Dhabi, and Doha.

top.title: Moderate House Democrats introduce rival Iran war powers resolution
top.description: Six centrist Democrats proposed a 30-day deadline for Iran operations, splitting from a stricter bipartisan measure headed to a floor vote Thursday.

top.title: Syria deploys thousands of troops to Lebanon border
top.description: The buildup, which began in February and accelerated recently, aims to block Hezbollah infiltration, according to Reuters.

top.title: CIA working to arm Kurdish forces for Iran uprising
top.description: The effort aims to spark an uprising as the U.S.-Israeli military campaign against Iran enters its second week with no clear endgame.

top.title: Iran threatens to attack any ship in Strait of Hormuz as Asia scrambles for energy
top.description: Asian governments are racing to secure alternative oil and gas supplies after Iran declared the strait closed, sending LNG prices surging.

top.title: Dr. Oz opens fraud probe into New York's Medicaid program
top.description: The CMS administrator sent Gov. Hochul 50 questions about cost controls and provider oversight in the state's $124 billion program.

top.title: Iran's Assembly of Experts elects Mojtaba Khamenei as next supreme leader
top.description: The slain leader's son was reportedly chosen under Revolutionary Guard pressure, even as Israeli airstrikes struck the vote site in Qom.

top.title: Sudan accuses Ethiopia of drone attacks in its civil war
top.description: Khartoum says drones launched from Ethiopian territory struck targets inside Sudan, the first time it has directly implicated its neighbor in the conflict.

top.title: Iranian drone strikes US Consulate in Dubai as war widens across Gulf
top.description: The attack came as Iran targeted American embassies and military bases across the region in retaliation for the killing of Ayatollah Khamenei.

top.title: Israel says it destroyed secret Iranian nuclear site near Tehran
top.description: The IDF said it struck a covert underground facility where scientists were developing nuclear weapons components, the first such site revealed since June 2025.

top.title: Trump says worst outcome in Iran is a new leader 'as bad' as Khamenei
top.description: The president acknowledged most U.S.-identified successors to the slain supreme leader have already been killed in ongoing strikes.

top.title: Macron deploys aircraft carrier to Mediterranean amid Middle East crisis
top.description: France ordered the Charles de Gaulle carrier group to the Mediterranean and deployed air defenses to Cyprus after a drone struck a British base there.

top.title: Lutnick volunteers to testify on Epstein ties
top.description: The commerce secretary's offer comes as lawmakers were preparing a bipartisan subpoena to compel his appearance before the House Oversight Committee.

top.title: Top U.S. general details timeline of Iran strikes
top.description: General Dan Caine outlined how the coordinated U.S.-Israel assault on Iran unfolded, as the conflict enters its fourth day with four U.S. troops killed.

top.title: Ukraine refuses EU requests to inspect damaged Druzhba pipeline
top.description: Kyiv denied access to the EU ambassador and top EU leaders, citing security concerns, as Hungary leverages the dispute to block a €90 billion loan.

top.title: FCC chair says Paramount-WBD merger will be approved 'quickly'
top.description: FCC Chairman Brendan Carr told CNBC the deal is "cleaner" than Netflix's earlier bid, signaling a smoother regulatory path for the $110 billion acquisition.

top.title: Trump orders Navy escorts, federal insurance for Gulf tankers
top.description: The president directed the U.S. Development Finance Corporation to offer political risk coverage for ships transiting the Strait of Hormuz amid the Iran conflict.

top.title: Apple unveils six devices in two days, more expected
top.description: Pre-orders for the M5 MacBook Air, M5 Pro/Max MacBook Pro, new Studio Displays, iPhone 17e, and M4 iPad Air open March 4.

top.title: Oil tops $82 as Iran conflict threatens Strait of Hormuz
top.description: Brent crude hit its highest level since January 2025 as the U.S.-Israeli military campaign against Iran disrupted shipping through a vital global oil chokepoint.

top.title: GOP Sen. Tillis calls for Noem's resignation at hearing
top.description: The North Carolina Republican invoked Noem's memoir about killing her dog to illustrate what he called a pattern of failed leadership at DHS.

top.title: IAEA confirms damage at Iran's Natanz nuclear site
top.description: The U.N. nuclear watchdog said satellite imagery shows entrance buildings were hit but no radiological consequences are expected.

top.title: Supreme Court lets California schools tell parents about child's gender identity
top.description: The 6-3 ruling reinstates a federal injunction blocking state laws that limited schools from disclosing students' transgender status to parents.

top.title: DOJ reverses itself, seeks to revive Trump law firm cases
top.description: Hours after moving to drop appeals of rulings that struck down Trump's executive orders targeting four law firms, the Justice Department reversed course.

top.title: Israel sends ground forces into southern Lebanon amid Hezbollah clashes
top.description: The IDF said troops seized strategic positions beyond the border to create a security buffer as fighting with Hezbollah escalates alongside the broader Iran conflict.

top.title: Ukraine says it recaptured more land than Russia seized in February
top.description: Kyiv's top general called it the first month Ukraine outpaced Russia in territorial gains since the 2024 Kursk incursion.

top.title: Judge rules Trump bid to kill NYC congestion pricing unlawful
top.description: U.S. District Judge Lewis Liman found the Transportation Department's 2025 move to terminate Manhattan's first-in-the-nation tolling program exceeded its authority.

top.title: Judge blocks ICE visit restrictions for all of Congress
top.description: A federal judge struck down DHS's seven-day notice requirement for the third time, now extending the ruling to protect all lawmakers, not just the 13 who sued.

top.title: House panel releases Clinton's Epstein deposition videos
top.description: More than nine hours of footage show Bill and Hillary Clinton answering questions about Jeffrey Epstein after months of legal battles.

top.title: Russia's Medvedev warns of World War III over US strikes on Iran
top.description: The deputy chairman of Russia's Security Council called Trump's regime-change campaign "a grave mistake" that has put all Americans at risk.

top.title: Israel to reopen Kerem Shalom crossing for Gaza aid
top.description: COGAT announced the key cargo crossing will reopen Tuesday after a days-long closure triggered by joint U.S.-Israeli strikes on Iran.

top.title: Supreme Court blocks redrawing of NYC's only GOP district
top.description: The ruling preserves Rep. Nicole Malliotakis's Staten Island-based seat for 2026, halting a state court order that found the map dilutes minority votes.

top.title: Noem refuses to retract 'terrorist' label for slain citizens
top.description: At a Senate hearing, the DHS secretary denied calling Alex Pretti a "domestic terrorist," saying she described the incident, not the person.

top.title: Rubio says Israel's strike plan triggered US attack on Iran
top.description: The secretary of state said the U.S. struck Iran preemptively after learning Israel would attack, fearing Tehran's automatic retaliation against American forces.

top.title: Bolivia raids homes to recover cash looted from deadly plane crash
top.description: Security forces searched at least 22 homes near El Alto, where a military plane carrying $62 million in new banknotes crashed last week, killing 22.

top.title: Trump says he'll attend Correspondents' Dinner for first time as president
top.description: The announcement ends a years-long boycott of the annual press dinner, which Trump skipped throughout his entire first term.

top.title: Montana Rep. Zinke won't seek reelection, adding to GOP retirement wave
top.description: The former Navy SEAL and Trump Interior secretary cited lingering military injuries, becoming the latest of more than 30 House Republicans to step aside.

top.title: Saudi Arabia goes on full military alert after Iranian drone strikes
top.description: The GCC condemned Iran's attacks across all six member states and invoked its joint defense pact, reserving the right to retaliate.

top.title: Trump won't rule out ground troops in Iran as strikes enter third day
top.description: Six U.S. service members have been killed in Iranian retaliatory strikes as the Pentagon declines to outline an exit strategy.

top.title: Sanders and Khanna introduce 5% wealth tax on billionaires
top.description: The bill would tax America's 938 billionaires to fund $3,000 direct payments to most households and expand social programs.

top.title: Navy intel chief warns China could strike US from its own waters by 2030s
top.description: New submarines armed with advanced ballistic missiles will be able to target large portions of the U.S. from Chinese coastal waters, Rear Adm. Brookes told Congress.

top.title: Netanyahu lobbied Trump for months to launch Iran war
top.description: The detailed account describes the U.S. decision to strike Iran as a major victory for Netanyahu, who first raised the idea in December.

top.title: Supreme Court hears case on gun ban for marijuana users
top.description: The justices weighed whether the federal law barring "unlawful" drug users from possessing firearms violates the Second Amendment.

top.title: Macron offers to station French nuclear weapons in Europe
top.description: France will expand its arsenal and extend "forward deterrence" to European allies, marking the biggest shift in French nuclear doctrine in decades.

top.title: Rubio says U.S. to roll out energy cost relief amid Iran oil crisis
top.description: Treasury and Energy secretaries will unveil measures Tuesday to cushion Americans from surging fuel prices after Iran conflict disrupted global oil supply.

top.title: Putin calls Gulf leaders as Iran conflict widens
top.description: Russia says it is in "constant contact" with Tehran and Gulf states as U.S.-Israeli strikes on Iran enter a third day.

top.title: Three US F-15s shot down by Kuwaiti air defenses in friendly fire incident
top.description: All six crew members ejected safely and are in stable condition, CENTCOM said, as the Iran conflict enters its fourth day.

top.title: Treasury ends Anthropic AI use as agencies comply with Trump ban
top.description: Federal housing agencies also cut ties with the AI company, which refused to drop safety guardrails on surveillance and autonomous weapons for the Pentagon.

top.title: Epstein files show DOJ examined accused men but charged none
top.description: Newly released documents reveal victims cooperated with investigators after Epstein's 2019 death, but prosecutors cited insufficient evidence to bring cases.

top.title: CIA tracked Khamenei for months before Israel struck Tehran
top.description: U.S. intelligence identified a rare gathering of Iran's top leaders, enabling the strike that killed the supreme leader and over 40 senior officials.

top.title: Trump pushes Venezuela model for Iran transition
top.description: The president told The New York Times he has "three very good choices" for Iran's next leader but declined to name them.

top.title: Epstein files show doctors shared women's health records with him
top.description: More than 15,000 newly released documents reveal how elite physicians bent ethical rules to serve the sex offender and the women he controlled.

top.title: US confirms first combat use of drone reverse-engineered from Iran's Shahed
top.description: The LUCAS kamikaze drone, built by copying a captured Iranian Shahed-136, was deployed in Operation Epic Fury strikes on Iranian military targets.

top.title: Iran's foreign minister vows 'no limit' to self-defense after Khamenei killed
top.description: Araghchi rejected Trump's warning against retaliation, saying Iran's military capability is intact and its forces are already striking back.

top.title: UN Security Council holds emergency session on US-Israel strikes against Iran
top.description: Iran retaliated with missile attacks across the region after the joint operation dubbed "Operation Epic Fury" targeted sites in multiple Iranian cities.

top.title: OpenAI reaches deal to deploy AI on Pentagon's classified networks
top.description: The agreement, announced by CEO Sam Altman, includes safety red lines on mass surveillance and autonomous weapons that rival Anthropic fought for but couldn't secure.

top.title: Businesses demand $175B in tariff refunds as Trump touts economy in Texas
top.description: A coalition of 950 businesses pressed the president during his Corpus Christi visit, saying Texas small businesses alone are owed $11 billion in unlawful tariff costs.

top.title: Saudi, UAE leaders unite against Iran after missiles rock Gulf capitals
top.description: Saudi Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman pledged "full solidarity" with UAE President Sheikh Mohammed bin Zayed after Iranian strikes killed one in Abu Dhabi.

top.title: DOJ won't appeal ruling barring death penalty for Mangione
top.description: Federal prosecutors said Friday they will not challenge a judge's decision removing the death penalty from Luigi Mangione's case, clearing the way for trial.

top.title: Trump launched Iran strikes despite intel doubting his missile threat claims
top.description: Intelligence sources told Reuters that Trump's claim Iran could "soon" hit the U.S. with missiles was unsupported by assessments putting that capability a decade away.

top.title: Houthis to resume Red Sea attacks after U.S.-Israeli strikes on Iran
top.description: Two senior officials told the AP the group's first strike could come as soon as "tonight," ending a ceasefire with Washington.

top.title: Chile bars Chinese hospital ship from treating locals amid US tensions
top.description: The decision came as Washington ramps up pressure on Santiago over a proposed Chinese undersea cable project.

top.title: Trump asks if Supreme Court can rehear tariff case
top.description: The president called the 6-3 ruling "highly disappointing" and warned it could force refunds of hundreds of billions to foreign companies.

top.title: Iran's defense chief, IRGC commander killed in US-Israeli strikes
top.description: Joint US-Israeli military operations struck Tehran and other Iranian cities Saturday, killing top military leaders and triggering Iranian missile retaliation.

top.title: Pentagon bars troops from Ivy League graduate programs
top.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth ordered the cancellation of all military attendance at Princeton, Columbia, MIT, Brown, Yale, and other elite schools starting next academic year.

top.title: Classified report found Noem's shoe policy created airport security risks
top.description: A DHS inspector general report described risks from the policy as "significant," according to The Wall Street Journal, adding to scrutiny of Noem's leadership.

top.title: Russia condemns US-Israeli strikes on Iran, evacuates citizens
top.description: Moscow labeled the joint military operation "unprovoked armed aggression," warned of radiological catastrophe, and began evacuating Russians from Iran.

top.title: Ghana says 55 citizens killed fighting for Russia in Ukraine
top.description: Foreign Minister Ablakwa said 272 Ghanaians were lured into the conflict since 2022 through criminal trafficking networks, with two held as POWs.

top.title: Trump says he's considering Cruz for Supreme Court
top.description: The president revived the idea at a Texas rally, despite Cruz rejecting it last month, saying "hell no" to any nomination.

top.title: FAA to cut O'Hare flights, citing overscheduling by major airlines
top.description: United Airlines and American Airlines have dramatically ramped up summer schedules at the Chicago hub, prompting federal intervention.

top.title: Slovak PM says Ukraine unwilling to restart Druzhba oil flow
top.description: Slovakia's prime minister spoke with Zelenskyy on Friday but said Kyiv rejected a joint inspection proposal, deepening a dispute that has stalled EU sanctions and aid.

top.title: Iran's top security chief rejects talks with US as strikes enter third day
top.description: Ali Larijani said Tehran "will not negotiate" with Washington, rebuffing Trump's claim that Iran's post-Khamenei leadership wanted dialogue.

top.title: Pentagon laser accidentally downs a Border Patrol drone in Texas
top.description: The military mistakenly shot down a CBP drone near Fort Hancock, marking the second border laser incident this month and drawing Democratic accusations of incompetence.

top.title: DOJ indicts 30 more over anti-ICE church protest in Minnesota
top.description: Attorney General Bondi said 25 of the newly charged defendants have been arrested, bringing the total number indicted in the case to 39.

top.title: Judge blocks early end of SAVE student loan plan
top.description: A federal judge dismissed the Trump administration's bid to eliminate the SAVE repayment plan, keeping the program alive for over 7 million borrowers.

top.title: Satellite images reveal US military buildup at Saudi airbase
top.description: Aircraft at Prince Sultan Airbase jumped from 27 to 43 over four days in February as the US ramps up its largest Middle East deployment in two decades.

top.title: ACLU sues Kansas over law invalidating transgender IDs
top.description: The lawsuit challenges SB 244, which retroactively voided driver's licenses and birth certificates of transgender residents who had legally updated their gender markers.

top.title: Trump denies knowledge of draft emergency order on midterm elections
top.description: The president said he had "never heard about" a proposal to declare a national emergency to reshape voting rules before November's midterms.

top.title: US birthrate hits record low, driven by teens and young women
top.description: Nearly half of American women at age 30 are now childless, as the steepest fertility declines fall on the youngest and least affluent.

top.title: NASA overhauls Artemis moon program, adds test flight before lunar landing
top.description: Administrator Isaacman said a new 2027 orbital mission will test commercial landers before attempting up to two moon landings in 2028.

top.title: Court lets Trump end union bargaining for federal workers
top.description: The Ninth Circuit vacated an injunction blocking the president's executive order, which strips collective bargaining rights from hundreds of thousands of employees.

top.title: OpenAI raises record $110B from Amazon, Nvidia, SoftBank
top.description: The funding round, at a $730 billion valuation, is the largest private financing deal ever and includes a sweeping Amazon partnership.

top.title: Zelenskyy says he's ready to meet Putin for peace talks
top.description: In a Sky News interview, the Ukrainian president said there is a closing window to end the war before U.S. midterm elections in November.

top.title: Trump tells homebuyers to 'wait a little longer' for lower mortgage rates
top.description: The president urged patience on housing costs as rates hit a three-year low but sales slumped to their weakest pace in over two years.

top.title: Asian shares fall as Nvidia slump and Pakistan-Afghanistan war rattle markets
top.description: Investors fled to safe havens Friday as tech valuations sagged post-Nvidia earnings and Pakistan declared "open war" on Afghanistan.

top.title: Nintendo plans $1.9B share sale as banks unwind stakes
top.description: MUFG Bank and the Bank of Kyoto are expected to sell their strategic holdings in the gaming giant, with a buyback also planned.

top.title: Senate Democrats demand tariff refunds, accuse Bessent of 'stonewalling'
top.description: More than two dozen senators urged the Treasury to begin repaying billions in duties the Supreme Court struck down last week.

top.title: Nvidia drops sharply despite record earnings as Block cuts half its staff
top.description: Wall Street sold off Thursday as Nvidia's blowout quarter failed to satisfy investors, while Block announced 4,000 layoffs tied to AI.

top.title: Green Party wins first-ever UK by-election, pushing Labour to third
top.description: Hannah Spencer captured the Gorton and Denton seat in Greater Manchester, overturning a Labour majority in a district the party held for nearly a century.

top.title: Trump floats 'friendly takeover of Cuba' amid oil blockade
top.description: The president said Cuba is "in a big deal of trouble" and that Secretary of State Rubio is negotiating at a "very high level."

top.title: Germany votes to let military intercept drones
top.description: The Bundestag passed a law allowing the Bundeswehr to jam or shoot down drones, responding to a surge in suspicious flights linked to Russia.

top.title: Venezuela suspends 19 Maduro-era oil contracts
top.description: Caracas and Washington are reviewing the deals and may revoke some, as both governments scrutinize the credentials of little-known firms involved.

top.title: Meloni calls Trump tariffs a 'mistake,' proposes EU-US free-trade zone
top.description: Italy's prime minister urged the EU to push for zero tariffs with Washington as the Turnberry trade deal remains frozen after a Supreme Court ruling.

top.title: White House confirms American killed in Cuba speedboat shootout
top.description: At least one U.S. citizen died and another was arrested after Cuban forces fired on a stolen Florida boat whose occupants allegedly shot first.

top.title: Maduro asks judge to toss drug indictment over blocked legal fees
top.description: His lawyer argues the U.S. violated Maduro's right to counsel by blocking Venezuela from paying his defense costs.

top.title: GOP senators join Democrats demanding DOJ release Epstein files tied to Trump
top.description: Bipartisan pressure mounted Thursday after an NPR investigation found FBI interview summaries involving accusations against the president were missing from the public release.

top.title: Syria, Druze factions complete first prisoner swap since summer clashes
top.description: The exchange of 86 detainees in Sweida marks a rare step toward political resolution in one of Syria's last autonomous regions.

top.title: 171 bodies found in mass graves near Congo's Uvira after M23 withdrawal
top.description: Officials say two graves were discovered in areas the Rwanda-backed rebel group recently vacated amid escalating violence in eastern Congo.

top.title: NYC mayor pitches Trump on $21B housing plan in Queens
top.description: Mamdani presented a mock Daily News front page reading "Trump to City: Let's Build" to sell the proposal, and Trump was "very enthusiastic," his office said.

top.title: Russia says it downed 220 Ukrainian drones over nine hours
top.description: The interceptions over nine hours included 27 drones heading for Moscow, according to Russian officials, amid escalating aerial exchanges on both sides.

top.title: ICE struggles to vet recruits amid hiring surge, internal email shows
top.description: An internal email warned supervisors that delayed background checks are creating confusion as some recruits were flagged for gang ties and active warrants.

top.title: US and Congo sign $1.2B health deal amid African data-sharing concerns
top.description: The DRC became the 19th country to sign a bilateral health agreement under Trump's "America First Global Health Strategy," even as the Africa CDC raised alarm over data provisions.

top.title: UN denounces Taliban decree expanding executions and curbing rights
top.description: Volker Türk condemned a new Taliban decree expanding the death penalty and legalizing corporal punishment, likening women's treatment to gender apartheid.

top.title: US offers $10M reward for tips leading to capture of Sinaloa Cartel leaders
top.description: The State Department announced bounties for René and Alfonso Arzate-García as a superseding indictment charges them with narcoterrorism.

top.title: FBI's congressional affairs chief departs amid ongoing bureau turmoil
top.description: Marshall Yates, a key liaison between the FBI and Congress who helped carry out the Trump administration's retribution efforts, is leaving this week.

top.title: Google rolls out Nano Banana 2 as default AI image model across Gemini
top.description: The upgrade brings Pro-level image generation to all users at faster speeds, spanning the Gemini app, Google Search, and 141 countries.

top.title: US saw net negative migration in 2025, a first since the Great Depression
top.description: A Wall Street Journal investigation found at least 180,000 Americans relocated abroad last year as the nation lost more people than it gained.

top.title: White House says Trump open to Kim talks 'without preconditions'
top.description: The response came hours after Kim Jong Un signaled willingness to engage at the close of North Korea's most significant political congress in five years.

top.title: Hillary Clinton's Epstein deposition paused after Boebert leaks photo
top.description: Rep. Lauren Boebert allegedly gave an unauthorized photo from the closed-door hearing to commentator Benny Johnson, who posted it online.

top.title: Stellantis cuts UAW profit-sharing for first time in 15 years after record loss
top.description: The automaker reported a net loss of roughly $26.3 billion for 2025, driven by massive EV writedowns and a sweeping strategic reset.

top.title: Columbia student detained by ICE freed after NYC mayor raised case with Trump
top.description: Ellie Aghayeva, a senior from Azerbaijan, was arrested from a campus building after agents allegedly posed as missing-person investigators.

top.title: US, Ukraine meet in Geneva after Russia's massive overnight aerial barrage
top.description: Talks focused on a postwar "prosperity package" and preparations for trilateral negotiations with Russia expected in early March.

top.title: Rutgers study challenges theory that melting glaciers could slow climate change
top.description: Meltwater from an Antarctic ice shelf delivers far less iron to the ocean than assumed, undermining a key natural climate feedback mechanism.

top.title: Trump surgeon general pick lacks GOP votes after vaccine grilling
top.description: At least two Republican senators remain undecided on Casey Means after she sidestepped vaccine questions at her confirmation hearing.

top.title: US rare earth shortages deepen as suppliers halt production
top.description: Two North American aerospace coating firms have suspended operations and begun rationing yttrium amid a 69-fold price surge over the past year.

top.title: UN issues first carbon credits under Paris Agreement
top.description: The milestone approval involves a clean-cooking project in Myanmar, though environmental groups have questioned the methodology behind the credits.

top.title: US aims to process 4,500 white South African refugees per month
top.description: A contracting document reveals the White House's monthly target far exceeds Trump's annual refugee cap of 7,500 for all nationalities.

top.title: Space Force halts Vulcan rocket launches over booster anomaly
top.description: The suspension could last "many months," jeopardizing a GPS satellite launch next month and ULA's plans to fly up to 22 Vulcan missions this year.

top.title: DARPA awards RTX contract for kilometer-range X-ray vision
top.description: The XENA program aims to let military commanders identify concealed threats from nearly a kilometer away, far beyond current X-ray scanner range.

top.title: Trump admin defers $259M in Medicaid funds from Minnesota
top.description: Vice President Vance and CMS Administrator Dr. Oz announced the action as part of a new "war on fraud" declared during Trump's State of the Union.

top.title: Nearly blind refugee found dead in Buffalo after Border Patrol left him
top.description: Nurul Amin Shah Alam, 56, went missing for six days after agents dropped him off without contacting his family or lawyer. Buffalo's mayor called the death "preventable."

top.title: NASA's Fincke says he was behind historic ISS medical evacuation
top.description: The veteran astronaut broke weeks of silence, revealing he experienced a "medical event" on Jan. 7 that cut the Crew-11 mission short.

top.title: Labor Dept. proposes rule easing gig worker classification
top.description: The proposal would rescind a Biden-era standard and make it easier for companies to treat workers as independent contractors.

top.title: Canada sends $8M in food aid to Cuba amid US fuel blockade
top.description: Mexico also dispatched a second shipment of humanitarian supplies as the island faces what the UN has warned could be a humanitarian collapse.

top.title: Bipartisan lawmakers press Navy to finalize 15-submarine deal
top.description: Congress warns that further delays to the multiyear contract for Virginia-class and Columbia-class boats could destabilize the nuclear submarine industrial base.

top.title: Patel fires FBI staff tied to Trump probe, says phone records seized
top.description: The FBI director ordered the ouster of at least 10 employees and revealed the bureau had subpoenaed his and Susie Wiles' call records during the Biden era.

top.title: Largest-ever ALMA image reveals Milky Way's hidden core
top.description: The mosaic spans more than 650 light-years, mapping cold star-forming gases and dozens of molecules near the galaxy's central black hole.

top.title: Larry Summers to resign from Harvard over Epstein ties
top.description: The former Harvard president and Treasury secretary will give up his University Professorship, the school's highest faculty distinction, at the end of the academic year.

top.title: Trump says Omar and Tlaib should be 'sent back' after State of the Union clash
top.description: The president called the two Muslim American lawmakers "lunatics" on Truth Social a day after they heckled him during his address to Congress.

top.title: Environmental groups sound alarm after Colorado River talks miss another deadline
top.description: Seven western states failed to agree on sharing the river's dwindling supply before current rules expire, as historic snow drought drains reservoirs.
